title: [Bug]: Subject-aware observability certification helper fails mypy policy construction
summary: The new subject-aware observability certification helper initially constructed ObservabilityPolicy with plain dictionaries in a way that passed runtime validation but failed strict static type checking.
observed: The full local check stopped during mypy validation for the subject-aware observability certification helper.
expected: The certification helper should use typed policy construction that passes mypy while preserving runtime validation and the public certification behavior.
reproduction: Run scripts/check.sh on the issue branch after adding the subject-aware observability certification helper.
impact: The issue blocks release validation for the new certification helper but does not affect released package behavior.
delivery_semantics: The fix is limited to test-helper policy construction and must not change ACK ordering, retries, DLQ publication, sink writes, or idempotency behavior.
security: The fix keeps the subject-aware observability certification path strict and typed without adding real subjects, endpoints, credentials, payloads, or operational identifiers to test evidence.
acceptance: The subject-aware observability certification helper passes mypy.
The focused subject-aware observability certification tests continue to pass.
No delivery behavior changes are introduced.
tests: Run the focused subject-aware observability certification suite, the observability regression suite, and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No user-facing documentation change is required beyond the issue #127 certification documentation already being added.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with sanitized test evidence.
